name: Excessive Database Connections
notes: Rule detects an excessive number of successful database connections.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Connections with the same source IP more than 60 times
conditions_text: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Connections with the same source IP more than 60 times, across exactly 1 destination IP within 1 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Connections with the same source IP more than 60 times, across exactly 1 destination IP within 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Database Connections on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Connections with the same source IP more than 60 times, across exactly 1 destination IP within 1 minutes
RED_list: Event description: An excessive number (more than 1 per second) of successful database connections have been made to this host.  This may be a false alarm on some very busy servers. However, it can often also be caused by poorly written applications that are wasting resources continually connecting to the database for every query.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
